ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general zucchini food product entry; no specific entry for seared zucchini with soy glaze and almond vinaigrette. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers cooked courgette/zucchini as closest parent; no specific code for seared preparation with soy glaze and almond vinaigrette.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific prepared vegetable dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of seared zucchini is approximately 180 g, comprising seared zucchini slices topped with soy glaze, almond vinaigrette, and fresh herbs.

nutrient_source.title: Zucchini, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170490); supplemented with soy sauce (FDC ID 172238) and almond (FDC ID 170567) component estimates
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for seared zucchini with soy glaze and almond vinaigrette in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked zucchini (FDC ID 170490) as the dominant component (~70% by weight), soy sauce (FDC ID 172238) for the glaze contribution (~10%), and whole almonds (FDC ID 170567) for the vinaigrette contribution (~10%), with olive oil (~10%) for the dressing base. Values are weighted composites rounded to one decimal place. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for non-starchy cooked vegetables
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for seared zucchini with soy glaze and almond vinaigrette. GI of 15 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care) values for cooked non-starchy vegetables (zucchini/courgette), which consistently report GI in the range of 10–20. The soy glaze and almond vinaigrette components are low-GI and do not materially raise the composite GI. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (15 × 9.7) / 100 ≈ 1.5. Classified as low GI (≤55) per Veritas threshold rules.

hypertension.guidance: The soy glaze contributes approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium soy sauce or limit portion size to manage daily sodium intake below 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index and low glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 15 and a glycemic load of 1.5 per serving, seared zucchini is an excellent choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it produces minimal postprandial blood glucose elevation; the almond vinaigrette further slows gastric emptying due to its healthy fat content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Predominantly monounsaturated fat from almonds and olive oil; low saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The almond vinaigrette and olive oil dressing provide monounsaturated fatty acids (approximately 2.6 g per 100 g) with low saturated fat (0.5 g per 100 g), supporting a heart-healthy lipid profile; however, the moderate sodium from soy glaze warrants attention for individuals with concurrent hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Seared Zucchini with Soy Glaze, Almond Vinaigrette, and Herbs is a Japanese-influenced vegetable dish served at contemporary fusion restaurants in Singapore, notably at To;asta on Circular Road. The dish features zucchini (courgette) slices that are seared at high heat to develop a caramelised exterior and tender interior, then finished with a savoury umami-rich soy glaze, a nutty almond vinaigrette, and fresh aromatic herbs. The flavour profile combines the mild sweetness of zucchini with the saltiness of soy, the richness of almonds, and the brightness of vinegar and herbs. Nutritionally, the dish is low in calories (approximately 68 kcal per 100 g), low in carbohydrates (5.4 g per 100 g), and provides a moderate amount of plant-based protein (2.8 g per 100 g) and heart-healthy monounsaturated fats from the almond vinaigrette. The glycemic index is very low (approximately 15), making it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or following a low-GI dietary pattern. The primary clinical consideration is the sodium contribution from the soy glaze (approximately 310 mg per 100 g), which is relevant for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is vegetarian, vegan, and dairy-free, but contains soy and tree nut (almond) allergens. It is not gluten-free due to the soy sauce component. The dish is classified as minimally processed and aligns with plant-forward dietary guidelines.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for seared zucchini with soy glaze and almond vinaigrette exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for cooked zucchini, soy sauce, almonds, and olive oil. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database values for cooked non-starchy vegetables. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from input value 'Grains and staples' to 'Vegetables', which accurately reflects the primary ingredient (zucchini). 2. Cuisine tags 'Pasta' and 'Udon' from the input record are inconsistent with the dish and have been disregarded; the dish is classified as Japanese fusion. 3. Nutrient values are composite estimates from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero for plant-based dish with no hydrogenated fats; confirmed zero per USDA source components. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-based dish containing no animal products. 6. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero for this plant-based dish; zucchini and almonds do not provide meaningful vitamin D. 8. Sentinel value -1 is not used in this record; all fields are either estimated from approved sources or set to 0.0 with justification as noted above. 9. GI set to 15 as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for cooked non-starchy vegetables; gi_category is 'low' (GI ≤ 55 per Veritas threshold rules). 10. Soy sauce contains gluten (wheat); dish is not gluten-free. 11. Almond allergen present in vinaigrette component.
